Effect version: 3.19.19

/.src/missedPipeableOpportunity.ts(11,30): warning TS377050: This nested call structure has a pipeable form. `addOne(5).pipe(...)` represents the same call sequence in pipe style and may be easier to read. effect(missedPipeableOpportunity)
/.src/missedPipeableOpportunity.ts(15,30): warning TS377050: This nested call structure has a pipeable form. `addOne(10).pipe(...)` represents the same call sequence in pipe style and may be easier to read. effect(missedPipeableOpportunity)

/.src/missedPipeableOpportunity.ts(23,33): warning TS377050: This nested call structure has a pipeable form. `wrapInEffect(regularFunction(5)).pipe(...)` represents the same call sequence in pipe style and may be easier to read. effect(missedPipeableOpportunity)
/.src/missedPipeableOpportunity.ts(27,30): warning TS377050: This nested call structure has a pipeable form. `addOne(7).pipe(...)` represents the same call sequence in pipe style and may be easier to read. effect(missedPipeableOpportunity)


==== /.src/tsconfig.json (0 errors) ====
    {
      "compilerOptions": {
        "plugins": [
          {
            "name": "@effect/language-service",
            "pipeableMinArgCount": 1
          }
        ]
      }
    }
    

==== /.src/missedPipeableOpportunity.ts (5 errors) ====
    // @effect-diagnostics missedPipeableOpportunity:warning
    import { Effect } from "effect"
    
    // Helper functions that work with Effect
    const addOne = (n: number) => Effect.succeed(n + 1)
    const double = (e: Effect.Effect<number>) => Effect.map(e, (n) => n * 2)
    const multiplyBy = (n: number) => (e: Effect.Effect<number>) => Effect.map(e, (m) => m * n)
    const toString = (e: Effect.Effect<number>) => Effect.map(e, (n) => String(n))
    
    // Should trigger diagnostic: nested calls with innermost returning pipeable
    export const shouldReport1 = toString(double(addOne(5)))
                                 ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
!!! warning TS377050: This nested call structure has a pipeable form. `addOne(5).pipe(...)` represents the same call sequence in pipe style and may be easier to read. effect(missedPipeableOpportunity)
    
    // Should trigger diagnostic: 3-level nesting
    const triple = (e: Effect.Effect<number>) => Effect.map(e, (n) => n * 3)
    export const shouldReport2 = toString(triple(double(addOne(10))))
                                 ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
!!! warning TS377050: This nested call structure has a pipeable form. `addOne(10).pipe(...)` represents the same call sequence in pipe style and may be easier to read. effect(missedPipeableOpportunity)
    
    // Should report: two level nesting can benefit from pipe style
    export const shouldReport4 = double(addOne(5))
                                 ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
!!! warning TS377050: This nested call structure has a pipeable form. `addOne(5).pipe(...)` represents the same call sequence in pipe style and may be easier to read. effect(missedPipeableOpportunity)
    
    // Should report, but refactor to be wrapInEffect(regularFunction(5)).pipe(double)
    const regularFunction = (n: number) => n + 1
    const wrapInEffect = (n: number) => Effect.succeed(n)
    export const shouldNotReport2 = double(wrapInEffect(regularFunction(5)))
                                    ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
!!! warning TS377050: This nested call structure has a pipeable form. `wrapInEffect(regularFunction(5)).pipe(...)` represents the same call sequence in pipe style and may be easier to read. effect(missedPipeableOpportunity)
    
    // Should trigger diagnostic: proper pipeable chain with correct types
    const addHundred = (e: Effect.Effect<number>) => Effect.map(e, (n) => n + 100)
    export const shouldReport3 = triple(addHundred(double(addOne(7))))
                                 ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
!!! warning TS377050: This nested call structure has a pipeable form. `addOne(7).pipe(...)` represents the same call sequence in pipe style and may be easier to read. effect(missedPipeableOpportunity)
    
    // Should NOT report: already using pipe
    export const shouldNotReport3 = addOne(5).pipe(double, toString)
    export const shouldNotReport4 = addOne(5).pipe(double)
    export const shouldNotReport5 = addOne(5).pipe(multiplyBy(2))
    
    // Should trigger
    export const shouldReport = Effect.runPromise(Effect.log("Hello"))
